message:
test(fcp-core): pin LeaseExpiry, MigratableComputationState, and CapabilityVerifier predicate matrix

Lands three more pinning test files contributed by parallel agents
finishing the open beads in the current pin sweep. All three are
test-only — no production code changes.

- crates/fcp-core/tests/lease_expiry_contract.rs (bead
  flywheel_connectors-3t95r, "LeaseExpiry computation"):
  Pins the LeaseParams → Lease::expiry computation path. Confirms
  that issuance + duration produces the expected RFC-3339 expiry,
  that an explicit not_after overrides duration, and that purpose
  / signature-set / provenance fields are carried through verbatim.
  Imports the public Lease, LeaseParams, LeasePurpose, ObjectId,
  Provenance, SignatureSet, TailscaleNodeId, ZoneId surface plus
  fcp_cbor::SchemaId so the contract is exercised through the
  exported API rather than internal helpers.

- crates/fcp-core/tests/connector_state_transitions.rs (bead
  flywheel_connectors-cgfwt, "ConnectorState transitions"):
  Bead text named "Loaded → Activated → Running → Suspended →
  Terminated", but those literal variants don't exist in fcp-core;
  the NORMATIVE migration state machine is
  MigratableComputationState in connector_state.rs:589. The test
  pins the actual variant set and the documented transition
  graph, plus the internally-tagged serde shape, so a future state
  rename / variant retag fails loudly. Header comment records the
  bead-text vs. code-reality reconciliation for future readers.

- crates/fcp-core/tests/capability_verifier_predicate_matrix.rs
  (bead flywheel_connectors-5a3vn, "CapabilityVerifier predicate
  matrix"):
  Builds CapabilityToken values via fcp_crypto's
  CapabilityTokenBuilder + Ed25519SigningKey, then exercises the
  CapabilityVerifier predicate set across the
  CAPABILITY_TOKEN_CLOCK_SKEW_SECS boundary. Pins which
  combinations of CapabilityConstraints, CapabilityId, InstanceId,
  OperationId, and ZoneId produce Allow vs. each FcpError variant.
  Uses chrono::{Duration, Utc} for deterministic clock-relative
  expiries.

All three bring the corresponding beads closer to closed; the next
beads-sync commit will absorb the resulting JSONL drift.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
